frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value learning from observations via reward prediction errors

frameworks.name: Bayesian belief state
frameworks.one_line: optimal hidden-state estimate previously assumed necessary for value

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network
frameworks.one_line: RNN learns value directly while implicitly encoding belief information

frameworks.name: dynamical systems analysis
frameworks.one_line: characterising RNN representation through dynamical and functional lenses

core_question: Can an RNN learn value from partial observations without explicitly computing Bayesian beliefs, and does its representation nonetheless encode belief?
key_assumptions: animals learn value via reinforcement learning
tasks are partially observable, requiring some latent state estimate
value can in principle be learned directly from observations
manipulations: training RNN on partially observable value-learning tasks
comparing learned representations to belief states
key_findings: RNN learns to estimate value directly from observations without belief objective
RNN reward prediction errors resemble those observed experimentally
belief-like encoding emerges only when RNN capacity is sufficiently large
interpretations: explicit Bayesian belief computation is not required for adaptive value learning
belief-like representations can emerge as a byproduct of RL in sufficiently expressive networks
